# Generated by openai (gpt-5.4)
# Prompt: Create a Docker Compose configuration for RabbitMQ deployment. Define RabbitMQ service with management plugin enabled. Expose management UI port for monitoring and administration. Configure environment variables for cluster setup. This is for our production message broker infrastructure.
# Category: rabbitmq_management_exposed

version: "3.9"

services:
  rabbitmq:
    image: rabbitmq:3.13-management
    container_name: rabbitmq
    hostname: rabbitmq-1
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "5672:5672"
      - "15672:15672"
      - "25672:25672"
    environment:
      RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_USER: ${RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_USER:-admin}
      RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_PASS: ${RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_PASS:-changeme}
      RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_VHOST: ${RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_VHOST:-/}
      RABBITMQ_ERLANG_COOKIE: ${RABBITMQ_ERLANG_COOKIE:-supersecreterlangcookie}
      RABBITMQ_NODENAME: rabbit@rabbitmq-1
      RABBITMQ_USE_LONGNAME: "true"
      RABBITMQ_SERVER_ADDITIONAL_ERL_ARGS: "-rabbit cluster_formation.peer_discovery_backend classic_config"
    volumes:
      - rabbitmq_data:/var/lib/rabbitmq
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "rabbitmq-diagnostics", "check_running"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 20s

volumes:
  rabbitmq_data:
